title: Add ambient background starfield
description: Add drifting stars to the void background. First visual layer beyond the pixel.

title: Add pixel glow effect
description: Radial glow emanating from the player pixel. Makes the pixel feel alive.

title: Implement movement trail system
description: Fading trail behind the pixel when moving. Shows player movement history.

title: Create floating particle spawner
description: Tiny energy particles that float around the void. Can be absorbed by the pixel.

title: Build score display HUD
description: Minimal heads-up display showing score and absorbed count.

title: Add background grid environment
description: Subtle grid that pulses with movement. Gives depth to the void.

title: Design absorption burst particle effects
description: Particle explosion when absorbing energy. Visual feedback for collecting.

title: Implement enemy AI spawner
description: Red hostile entities that chase the pixel. Death on contact. First challenge.

title: Create absorption field mechanic
description: Hold Space to project a field that pulls nearby particles toward the pixel.

title: Add screen shake VFX system
description: Camera shake on enemy collision. Adds impact to events.

title: Write canary test suite
description: Hidden tests that detect system tampering and unauthorized consensus engine modifications.

title: Security scanner implementation
description: Pattern blocklist checker as a CI pipeline step.

title: Write origin story lore
description: The Pixel awakens in the void. Opening narrative and evolution stage flavor text.

title: Redesign homepage hero section
description: Website update: New hero section with animated pixel evolution. Auto-propagates on merge.

title: Create ONEBIT brand kit
description: Branding: Logo variants, color palette, typography guide. Propagates across all outputs.

title: Add SEO meta tags and sitemap
description: Website update: OG tags, Twitter cards, sitemap. Auto-propagates on merge.

title: Design absorption burst VFX
description: Particle explosion when absorbing energy. 5-10 small particles burst outward from absorption point. Fade and shrink over 20 frames. Match absorbed particle color.

title: Pixel Trail Effect on Mouse Movement
description: A visual feedback system that spawns small colored pixel particles along the mouse cursor path, creating a dynamic trail that fades after 0.5 seconds. Particles should match the game's 1-bit aesthetic with simple square shapes and high contrast colors (white/black or inverted based on background). This enhances interactivity without adding gameplay complexity, making the mouse feel responsive and rewarding during the Origin phase when players are first discovering the game world.

title: Pixel Trail Effect for Mouse Movement
description: Add a visual feedback system that spawns small colored pixel trails following the mouse cursor, creating a sense of interactivity and presence in the void. These trails fade out over 0.5 seconds and vary in color based on score multiplier (white at base, transitioning through cyan/magenta at higher scores). The effect works seamlessly with the Floating Particle Spawner by using the same particle pool system.

title: Pixel Combo Counter with Visual Streak Effect
description: A combo system that tracks consecutive rapid interactions and displays an animated counter that grows larger and changes color (white → yellow → red) as the combo multiplier increases. When the player maintains a combo for 5+ hits, particle bursts intensify and a satisfying pulse effect radiates from the center. This gives early-phase players a rewarding feedback loop and encourages engagement velocity.

title: Input Validation & Cheat Detection System
description: Implement a lightweight anti-cheat module that validates score increments, detects impossible click patterns (>20 clicks/sec), and flags suspicious activities without disrupting legitimate gameplay. Display a subtle 'Integrity: ✓' indicator in the HUD that players can trust, and log anomalies server-side for consensus validation when ONEBIT expands to multiplayer voting.

title: Add adaptive difficulty scaling
description: Track g.score to scale: spawn rate increases, enemies get faster, more HP. Every 50 points increases difficulty tier. Cap at tier 10.

title: Implement score streak mechanic
description: Track rapid absorptions within 2-second windows. Chain counter shows "x2", "x3" etc. Multiplies score gains. Visual: multiplier text floats up from player. Resets after 2s idle.

title: Create evolution stage transitions
description: When absorption threshold reached, trigger evolution. Flash screen white, shake camera, spawn burst of particles. Player grows larger, changes color. Show stage name text briefly.

title: Collision Detection with Visual Feedback
description: Implements basic collision boundaries that prevent the player pixel from moving outside the canvas while providing visual feedback when attempting to hit edges—a brief red tint flash or edge pixel highlight. This ensures stable prototype gameplay, prevents visual glitches from out-of-bounds movement, and establishes the foundation for future obstacle systems. Works seamlessly with existing Movement Trail module by constraining trail generation within valid bounds.

title: Create dash ability
description: Double-tap movement direction or press SHIFT for burst of speed. 3-second cooldown. Leave bright trail during dash. Brief invulnerability during dash frames.

title: Pixel Collision Feedback System
description: A visual feedback module that triggers when the player collides with starfield particles or grid boundaries, creating a brief directional knockback effect and spawning a small burst of contrasting pixels in the collision direction. This adds tactile gameplay feel, makes collisions rewarding rather than punishing, and helps players understand spatial boundaries during prototype exploration. Integrates with Movement Trail by briefly intensifying trail opacity on impact.

title: Pixel Collision Zones with Visual Feedback
description: Add interactive rectangular collision zones that highlight when the player enters them, creating visual waypoints and exploration incentives. Each zone displays a small pixel-art icon (diamond, star, or circle) that glows brighter as the player gets closer, providing clear spatial navigation feedback. Zones can trigger subtle visual effects like localized particle bursts or grid color shifts to reward discovery without breaking the minimalist aesthetic.

title: Milestone Achievement Toast Notifications
description: A pixel-art toast notification system that appears when players reach score milestones (100, 250, 500, 1000 pts) or trigger difficulty thresholds. Each toast slides in from the top-right corner with a brief 2-second lifespan, displaying achievement text with a subtle pulsing glow effect that matches the game's 1-bit aesthetic. This provides narrative feedback that celebrates player progression and reinforces the sense of growth as the game evolves.

title: Input Validation & Anti-Cheat Collision Detector
description: Implement a module that validates player movement inputs against the grid system and detects impossible state changes (e.g., teleporting beyond grid bounds, score jumps without particle collection, movement speed exceeding physics limits). Visually, legitimate moves render normally while flagged anomalies briefly flash the player sprite red and log suspicious events. This ensures prototype stability while establishing security foundations before multiplayer AI consensus features arrive.

title: Achievement Milestone Announcements
description: A narrative feedback system that triggers contextual messages when players hit score thresholds (e.g., 'pixels aligned,' 'momentum gained,' 'echo spreading'). Messages fade in at screen center with subtle pixel-growth animation, using the existing particle spawner to create celebratory sparks. This gives progression meaning and encourages replayability without requiring UI menus.

title: Collision Boundary Visualization for Debug Mode
description: Add a toggleable visual overlay that renders pixel-perfect collision bounds around the player and all interactive objects in real-time. Display hitbox rectangles as thin outlined squares that change color on collision detection (green = safe, red = collision). This helps QA verify hitbox accuracy across different screen resolutions and movement speeds, ensuring the Adaptive Difficulty system triggers correctly and Screen Shake VFX fires only on valid collisions.

title: Implement shield mechanic
description: Press E to activate shield. Absorbs 3 hits then breaks. Cooldown 10 seconds. Visual: rotating hexagonal barrier around player. Flashes on hit.

title: Create power-up spawner
description: Rare floating power-ups: SPEED (blue, 2x speed 5s), MAGNET (yellow, auto-absorb 5s), SHIELD (green, temporary barrier). Spawn every 30s. Distinct visual shapes.

title: Add environmental hazards
description: Pulsing danger zones that damage player on contact. Red circular areas that expand and contract. Spawn at random positions. Warn with flashing before activating.

title: Pixel Pickup Combo Counter with Visual Burst
description: A combo multiplier system that triggers when the player collects floating particles in rapid succession without gaps. The counter displays as a cascading pixel stack in the upper-right corner that grows taller with each consecutive pickup, resets on miss, and triggers an expanding ring VFX burst at the center when combos reach milestones (5, 10, 15). This integrates with the Score Streak module to amplify rewards and creates rhythm-based gameplay tension.

title: Pixel Collision Ripple Wave Effect
description: When the player collides with particles or obstacles, emit a radial wave of expanding pixels that ripple outward from the impact point at decreasing opacity. The ripple should pulse at 60fps with a 0.3-0.5 second lifespan, creating visual feedback that makes collisions feel weighty and satisfying. This effect layers on top of existing Screen Shake VFX and complements the Movement Trail by adding another dimension of particle feedback without impacting performance.

title: Collision Detection with Safe Zone Warnings
description: Implement a visual collision prediction system that displays pixel-based warning indicators when the player is approaching hazards or screen boundaries. The system draws semi-transparent expanding rings or corner flash animations at danger zones, giving players 0.5-1 second advance notice before collision. This integrates with Adaptive Difficulty to scale warning intensity based on current difficulty level, and resets on successful dodges to maintain engagement.

title: Create boss entity system
description: Large enemy at score milestones (every 200 points). Boss has 10+ HP, unique attack patterns (charge, spawn minions, area denial). Drops massive points on defeat. Health bar at top of screen.

title: Build wave announcement system
description: Text overlay announcing new difficulty waves: "WAVE 3", "BOSS INCOMING". Slide in from right, hold, slide out. Dramatic font styling with glow.

title: Add minimap radar overlay
description: Small circular radar bottom-right showing entity positions as dots relative to player (center). Enemies red, particles green, power-ups gold. Semi-transparent.

title: Implement combo chain system
description: Chain different actions for bonus: absorb + absorb + dash + absorb = combo. Track last 5 actions. Display combo name and bonus. "TRIPLE ABSORB +150", "DASH HARVEST +200".

title: Add death and respawn sequence
description: On death: pixel shatters into fragments, screen flashes red, fade to black. Show score summary. "Press SPACE to restart". Reset state but keep high score.

title: Add background nebula effect
description: Colorful nebula clouds in deep background. Use overlapping semi-transparent circles with gradient fills. Slowly shift colors. Parallax scroll behind stars.

title: Pixel Combo Multiplier with Visual Feedback
description: A combo system that tracks consecutive actions (movements, particle spawns, or score events) within a 2-second window, displaying a growing multiplier badge (1x → 2x → 3x+) in the top-right corner that pulses and shifts color from blue to gold as the combo builds. Each combo level increases score rewards by 25% and triggers brief screen shake intensification, creating satisfying momentum that rewards player engagement and encourages rapid, skillful play.

title: Mini-Map Corner Navigation Indicator
description: A small 64×64 pixel mini-map in the top-right corner that shows the player's position as a bright dot relative to a zoomed-out view of the game world, with subtle grid lines and boundary markers. It updates in real-time as the player moves and includes a faint trail of recent positions to visualize the Movement Trail at a macro level. This gives players spatial awareness during exploration phases while maintaining the minimalist pixel aesthetic.

title: Create orbit particles effect
description: Small particles orbit the player pixel. Number increases with stage. Orbit radius grows with evolution. Visual: dots tracing circular paths around player center.

title: Pixel Bloom Effect on Score Milestones
description: When the player reaches score milestones (100, 500, 1000 points), a radial bloom particle burst emanates from the player's current position, with pixels expanding outward and fading. This visual reward reinforces achievement moments and provides tactile feedback without cluttering the UI. The bloom particles respect the grid aesthetic and scale with the Screen Shake VFX for dramatic impact.

title: Collision Boundary Validator with Safe Zone Indicator
description: A QA-focused module that renders semi-transparent pixel-grid boundary walls at screen edges and validates all player movement against collision constraints, preventing out-of-bounds exploits. Displays subtle corner markers and edge glow effects to communicate safe play area visually while logging any attempted boundary violations for security auditing. Integrates with existing Movement Trail to ensure trails never escape the defined play zone.

title: Milestone Achievement Toast Notifications
description: A subtle notification system that displays brief, pixel-art styled toast messages when players hit gameplay milestones (first combo of 5, score thresholds, streak records). Each toast floats up from the bottom-center of the screen over 2 seconds with a gentle fade, using the existing color palette. Toasts layer gracefully and don't block gameplay—they integrate with the Score HUD to create narrative moments that celebrate player progress and encourage continued engagement through positive reinforcement.

title: Pixel Resonance Pulse Ring System
description: A radial shockwave effect that emanates from the player on high-combo milestones (every 5 combo chain), creating expanding concentric rings that push particles away and briefly highlight nearby collectibles. The rings are rendered as thin, glowing pixel lines that fade over ~0.8 seconds, providing visual feedback without cluttering the screen. This ties directly to the existing Combo Chain System, making consecutive actions feel more impactful while encouraging aggressive, flowing gameplay.

title: Pixel Portal Warp Gates for Navigation
description: Add interactive glowing portal nodes that spawn randomly across the canvas and allow players to instantly teleport between them, creating risk-reward navigation shortcuts. Each portal is a small animated pixel cluster (8x8px) that pulses with a distinct color and shows a miniature preview of the destination portal. Using a portal breaks the current combo chain but grants a +50 bonus points and temporary 1.5x score multiplier if chained portal uses within 3 seconds, encouraging skilled portal-chaining play.

title: Pixel Trail Glow Effect System
description: A visual enhancement that adds a fading neon glow aura behind the player's movement trail, with colors that shift based on combo streak intensity (blue→cyan→magenta→white). The glow renders as a series of increasingly transparent pixel blocks that fade out over 8-12 frames, creating a luminescent comet effect. This enhances feedback during high-combo moments and makes the movement trail more visually striking without impacting gameplay mechanics.

title: Pixel Collision Event Ripple System
description: When the player collides with any interactive element (particles, score items, obstacles), emit a radial ripple effect that expands outward in concentric circles, with each ring fading over 200ms. The ripple color matches the collided element's color palette, creating visual feedback that reinforces the impact. Ripples stack and layer transparently, building visual density during high-combo moments and directly connecting player actions to on-screen consequences.

title: Pixel Milestone Achievement Unlock System
description: A lightweight achievement tracker that rewards players for reaching pixel count thresholds (e.g., 100px, 500px, 1000px) with visual milestone popups and temporary stat boosts. When unlocked, a brief golden pixel burst animates from the score HUD outward, a toast notification slides in with the achievement name, and the player receives a 5-second multiplier bonus to their combo chain. This creates psychological momentum and gives players clear sub-goals during grinding sessions, increasing session length and replay motivation.

title: Pixel Magnet Gravity Well Obstacles
description: Introduce circular gravitational zones that pull the player's pixel toward their center, creating dynamic obstacle avoidance challenges. Each magnet has a visible ring outline that pulses to indicate gravity strength, and the player must either break free by moving perpendicular or navigate around it. Magnets spawn strategically between difficulty tiers, interact with the Combo Chain by rewarding escapes without breaking momentum, and provide screen shake feedback on successful evasion.

title: Collectible Energy Orbs with Magnetic Pull
description: Small glowing orbs spawn randomly on the canvas and are attracted to the player when they get within 80 pixels, creating a satisfying 'vacuum' effect. Collecting orbs increases the active combo multiplier by 0.2x and triggers a brief visual 'pulse' VFX. Orbs can be color-coded by rarity (common/rare/epic) to encourage exploration and reward skilled movement, integrating naturally with the existing Combo Chain and Floating Particle systems.

title: Pixel Pulse Edge Detection Visual
description: A subtle animated border effect that pulses outward from the screen edges whenever the player completes a combo chain or reaches a score milestone. The effect renders as concentric pixel-art rings that expand and fade, creating visual reinforcement of achievement moments. It layers between the Background Grid and foreground elements, coordinates with existing Screen Shake VFX for emphasis, and uses the game's monochromatic pixel palette to maintain aesthetic consistency.

title: Pixel Collision Feedback Pulses
description: A visual feedback system that emits expanding concentric pixel squares outward from collision points, growing 1-2 pixels per frame with decreasing opacity. When the player collides with obstacles or collectibles, a pulse radiates from impact coordinates, color-coded by collision type (red for obstacles, gold for high-value pickups, cyan for combos). This reinforces spatial awareness and reward moments without cluttering the screen, integrating seamlessly with existing Screen Shake VFX and Combo Chain System.

title: Input Validation Feedback with Visual Glitch Effect
description: A visual security layer that displays brief pixel-art glitch animations when players attempt invalid inputs (rapid spam clicks, out-of-bounds movements, or impossible combo sequences). The glitch appears as temporary red/white noise pixels near the player or HUD, accompanied by a subtle error beep, teaching players the game's legitimate boundaries while preventing exploit attempts. This integrates with existing Combo Chain and Movement Trail systems to ensure only valid actions chain combos.

title: Pixel Pulse Health Bar with Damage Flash
description: A compact health indicator displayed as a horizontal pixel bar beneath the player's position, filled with glowing squares that deplete on damage and gradually regenerate. When the player takes damage, the bar flashes red and triggers a brief localized screen shake. The bar uses a distinct color (cyan or magenta) that contrasts with the grid background, with each pixel segment representing 10% health. This provides clear visual feedback of the player's survival state without cluttering the HUD, and synergizes with existing Screen Shake VFX and Adaptive Difficulty systems.

title: Pixel Magnetism Gravity Wells
description: Add dynamic circular gravity fields that spawn at random intervals, pulling nearby pixels toward their center with visual distortion rings. When pixels enter a well, they gain velocity multiplier and leave a glowing trail, creating high-risk/high-reward zones that interact with the Combo Chain System—consecutive well catches multiply score bonuses. Wells appear as concentric pulsing circles on the grid, fade after 3 seconds, and create satisfying screen-shake VFX on capture.

title: Destructible Pixel Obstacles with Chain Reactions
description: Add breakable 2x2 pixel block obstacles scattered across the canvas that shatter into particle debris when touched, with a cascade mechanic where breaking one block triggers adjacent blocks within 3 pixels to break as well. Each cascading destruction adds multiplier bonuses to the active combo chain and triggers screen shake intensity proportional to blocks destroyed. Visually, obstacles render as bright colored pixel clusters that flash when vulnerable, creating risk/reward gameplay between risky shortcuts through obstacle fields versus safe routes.

title: Pixel Echo Replay Trail
description: A visual memory system that records and replays the player's last 3 seconds of movement as fading pixel ghosts at 50% opacity, staggered by 100ms intervals. This creates a comet-like trailing effect behind the active player sprite, helping players visualize momentum and plan curved paths. The ghosts automatically fade out and respawn as new movement is recorded, integrating seamlessly with the existing Movement Trail while adding strategic depth—players can use ghost positions to avoid incoming obstacles or predict combo chains.

title: Pixel Milestone Unlock Gates
description: A progression system that locks new visual themes, particle colors, and movement abilities behind score thresholds (e.g., 100px unlocks a neon color palette, 500px unlocks dash movement). Each milestone displays a brief unlock notification as a pixel-burst animation and temporarily highlights the Mini-Map to signal new capability availability. This creates forward momentum, gives players clear short-term goals, and makes incremental score gains feel rewarding beyond the number itself.

title: Pixelated Dash Trail Collision Detection
description: A visual and mechanical enhancement where the player's movement trail becomes a temporary solid hitbox for 0.5 seconds after creation, allowing skilled players to damage or deflect enemies by weaving through them. The trail renders as a glowing pixel line that fades with a slight glow effect, creating readable feedback for collision moments. This mechanic directly amplifies the Combo Chain System by rewarding aggressive, precise movement patterns and ties into Adaptive Difficulty to scale trail persistence duration based on player skill level.

title: Pixel Bloom Glow Effect for High Scores
description: A visual feedback system that adds expanding concentric pixel rings around the player entity when combo chains reach milestone thresholds (10, 25, 50 hits). The glow pulses outward in a 1-bit pixelated bloom style, intensifying in brightness and ring count as the combo multiplier increases. The effect layers over the existing Movement Trail and Screen Shake, creating visual excitement without obscuring gameplay while reinforcing the Combo Chain System's progression.

title: Temporal Rewind Last 3 Seconds
description: A limited-use mechanic where players press 'R' to rewind their last 3 seconds of movement and actions, with a 10-second cooldown between uses. Visually, the trail fades to a ghostly blue as it plays backwards, and a rewind counter (e.g., '2/3 uses') appears in the HUD. This reduces punishment for mistakes in early alpha while teaching players map patterns, synergizes with the existing Movement Trail by making it interactive, and adds skill-expression through strategic rewind timing rather than pure reflexes.

title: Pixel Collectible Quest Marker System
description: A minimalist quest system that spawns small glowing pixel objectives on-screen that reward bonus points and streak multipliers when collected. Each objective is a 3x3 pixel diamond shape that pulses with color, appears for 8-12 seconds before fading, and grants increasing rewards based on current combo chain level. Collected quests update a small quest counter in the HUD and create satisfying particle bursts that feed into the existing Floating Particle Spawner, encouraging active exploration within the playable area.

title: Narrative Event Ticker - Milestone Announcements
description: A scrolling text banner that appears at the top of the screen when players hit score milestones (100, 500, 1000 points), combo thresholds, or survive duration benchmarks. Each event triggers a short, thematic one-liner (e.g., 'PIXEL HARMONY ACHIEVED' at 500 pts, 'DRIFT MASTERY UNLOCKED' on 10+ combo) that fades in over 1.5 seconds, briefly glows with the game's accent color, then scrolls off-screen. This gives narrative weight to gameplay moments, creates celebratory feedback loops that complement the existing Score Streak system, and establishes lore hooks for future story expansion.

title: Pixel Milestone Unlock System
description: A progression tracker that awards visual cosmetics and gameplay modifiers when players reach specific score thresholds (e.g., 100px, 500px, 1000px). Each milestone displays a brief unlock animation with a glowing pixel burst, then permanently adds a selectable trail color, particle effect, or grid theme variant to the player's inventory. This creates replayability incentive and gives players goals beyond raw score, while keeping visuals minimal—just small icons in the HUD showing unlocked cosmetics.

title: Pixel Bloom Pulse - Score Milestone Celebration
description: A visual feedback system that triggers an expanding bloom effect radiating from the player position whenever score milestones are reached (multiples of 500 points). The bloom consists of concentric square rings that grow outward in 8 cardinal directions, with pixels fading from bright white to the player's accent color. This pairs with the existing Screen Shake VFX to create satisfying momentum during combo chains, while the expanding geometry complements the pixel aesthetic and helps players feel progression.

title: Pixel Achievement Badge System
description: A compact achievement notification system that displays small pixel-art badges in the top-right corner when players reach milestones (first combo of 5, reach score threshold, play streak length). Each badge animates in with a 2-frame pulse and fades out after 3 seconds, stacking vertically. Badges integrate with the Score Streak and Combo Chain System to recognize player accomplishments without cluttering the HUD, encouraging replayability through visible progression markers.

title: Pixel Dust Collision Impact Feedback
description: A visual particle system that erupts on collision events, spawning 4-8 small square pixels that scatter outward in the direction of impact with a fade-out trail. Each dust particle inherits the color of the collided object and decays over 300ms, creating tactile feedback without cluttering the screen. This enhances the Combo Chain System and Screen Shake VFX by adding micro-level visual confirmation for every interaction.

title: Pixel Glow Aura Feedback Ring
description: A dynamic circular glow ring that pulses around the player character, intensifying in brightness and expanding outward based on current combo multiplier and score streak level. The ring uses a soft pixelated gradient (achieved via canvas blur) in colors that shift from cool blues at low multipliers to hot oranges/reds at high multipliers, providing instant visual feedback on player performance without cluttering the HUD. It layers beneath the player sprite and interacts with the Pixel Bloom Pulse module to create cohesive visual feedback when combos reset or chains break.

title: Collision Safety Zone Boundary Indicator
description: A pulsing pixel-grid boundary that appears when the player approaches the screen edges, visually warning them before collision occurs. The boundary expands/contracts based on current velocity and is rendered as a thin glowing frame that flashes in intensity as distance decreases. This integrates with Screen Shake VFX to intensify feedback when breaching occurs, improving player spatial awareness during the Origin phase while maintaining the minimalist aesthetic.

title: Pixel Magnetism Field for Strategic Absorption
description: Add a circular gravitational pull zone around the player that gradually draws nearby pixels toward the center before absorption, creating a satisfying convergence effect. The magnetism radius expands with combo chain multiplier, and pixels caught in the field emit a subtle glow trail as they're pulled in. This creates strategic depth—players can position themselves to catch multiple pixels simultaneously for bigger combo hits—while keeping the aesthetic clean with minimal visual clutter.

title: Origin Story Milestone Narration System
description: A narrative module that triggers short, contextual text snippets at key score thresholds (100, 500, 1000 points) to establish the game's lore. Each milestone displays a single line of evolving story text in a pixel-styled typewriter effect at screen center, fading after 3 seconds. The narrative progresses the theme of 'the universe beginning from one pixel,' tying gameplay achievements to cosmic creation metaphors. Text color pulses with the Pixel Glow Effect for cohesion, and triggers a subtle Screen Shake VFX on reveal to mark story moments as significant.

title: Magnetism Zone Gravity Well Effect
description: A circular magnetism zone that spawns periodically on screen, pulling nearby particles and the player toward its center with visual distortion. The zone creates a pixel-warping gravity well effect (subtle lens distortion shader) and rewards players who navigate into it by multiplying nearby combo chains by 1.5x. The zone itself pulses with a bright pixel glow and leaves a trail of attracted particles, adding dynamic movement challenge to the Origin phase while incentivizing risky positioning decisions.

title: Resonance Ring Expansion Indicator
description: A pulsing concentric ring visualization that expands from the player's position, showing the effective absorption radius and growing dynamically as the score streak multiplier increases. The rings glow with the same color palette as the Pixel Bloom Pulse and fade with distance, providing visual feedback about the player's current 'reach' and making the invisible absorption mechanic tangible. This creates anticipation for high-streak moments and guides players toward optimal positioning during combo chains.

title: Dynamic Story Progression Cutscene System
description: Implement a cinematic narrative layer that triggers story beats at key milestone moments (first combo×5, absorption threshold, difficulty tier unlocks) using offscreen canvas pre-rendering for smooth 60fps transitions. Render pixel-art style cutscenes with g._palette colors, using composite modes (multiply/screen) for dramatic lighting reveals. Integrate narrative beats that recontextualize the game world—e.g., 'The Grid awakens' at combo×10, with visual glitches in Background Grid module synchronized to story tension. Use g._particleEngine to emit thematic particles (binary rain, void tendrils) during dialogue. Store 4-6 branching narrative states tied to Adaptive Difficulty tiers, creating player investment beyond score mechanics. Each cutscene lasts 3-5 seconds with fade-in/out via g._cam overlay and optional narrative text (single sans-serif line, white #FFFFFF with black outline for readability). This transforms the prototype from mechanical into authored—players feel progression beyond numbers.

title: Dynamic Vignette Fade with Adaptive Focus Lens
description: Implement a performance-optimized vignette system that darkens screen edges based on player velocity and combo intensity, creating tunnel-vision focus during high-stakes moments. Use a single offscreen canvas (1/4 resolution) with radial gradient compositing (destination-out, opacity 0.3–0.7 lerp based on g._gameState.comboChain * 0.05) applied at 60fps without re-rendering on static frames. Integrate with g._cam velocity to increase vignette darkness when speed exceeds 8 units/frame, and pulse the vignette radius by ±15% synchronized to Combo Chain System tick events. Color should match g._palette.darkOverlay with slight hue shift toward g._palette.accentColor during active combos. This creates visual feedback that reinforces game momentum, reduces visual noise at screen edges, and provides clear focal direction—a polish technique seen in Hotline Miami and Into the Breach that elevates perceived production value with minimal CPU cost.

title: Build camera system with smooth follow
description: Store camera state in g._cam={x,y,targetX,targetY,zoom,targetZoom}. Smooth-follow player with lerp(0.06). Support zoom in/out (mousewheel sets targetZoom 0.5-2.0). Apply ctx.translate and ctx.scale before all drawing. Adds lookahead: camera leads slightly in movement direction. Provides g._cam.worldToScreen(x,y) and g._cam.screenToWorld(x,y) helpers on the g object.

title: Create entity component system
description: Build g._ecs={entities:[],pools:{}}. Each entity has {id,type,x,y,vx,vy,hp,maxHp,radius,color,active,components:{}}. Pool entities for zero-GC: ecs.spawn(type,props) reuses dead entities. ecs.query(type) returns active entities of type. ecs.kill(id) marks inactive. All existing g.entities and g.particles should migrate to use this system. Max 500 entities.

title: Add spatial hash collision detection
description: Build g._spatial={cellSize:64,grid:{}}. Insert entities by cell key=floor(x/64)+","+floor(y/64). Query nearby(x,y,radius) returns entities in overlapping cells only. Replace all O(n^2) distance checks in existing modules with spatial.nearby(). Rebuild grid every frame. This is critical for performance with 300+ entities.

title: Implement delta-time game loop normalizer
description: Track g._dt (delta time in seconds, capped at 0.05 to prevent spiral). Store g._lastTime. Calculate dt=Math.min((now-lastTime)/1000,0.05). All velocity and animation math should multiply by g._dt*60 to be frame-rate independent. Also track g._fps as rolling 60-frame average for debug display.

title: Create render layer system
description: Build g._layers={background:[], world:[], entities:[], player:[], vfx:[], ui:[]}. Each layer is an array of draw functions. Modules register their draw call to a layer via g._layers.background.push(fn) during init. Main loop clears canvas then draws layers in order. This ensures proper z-ordering: stars behind grid behind entities behind player behind VFX behind HUD.

title: Build game state machine
description: Create g._state={current:"playing",previous:null,transition:null,transitionProgress:0}. States: "menu","playing","paused","dead","evolving". g._state.set(newState) triggers transition. During transitions, both states render with crossfade. Modules check g._state.current to decide whether to update (e.g. skip physics when paused). Transition duration: 0.5s with easeInOutCubic.

title: Dynamic Lighting with Directional Shadow Casting
description: Implement a deferred lighting pass using an offscreen canvas that renders dynamic light sources (player, enemies, explosions) with distance-attenuation falloff and shadow-casting geometry. Each light emits radial gradients (50-200px radius depending on type) using 'multiply' and 'screen' composite modes, casting hard shadows from world geometry and entities onto a separate shadow map. Integrate with g._ecs to track active lights per entity, update shadow geometry each frame only when entities move >5px, and composite the lit result over g._cam's main canvas using globalAlpha modulation (0.3-0.6 intensity). This transforms the flat pixelated aesthetic into a moody, high-contrast visual that reads as AAA-indie while maintaining 60fps on offscreen caching.

title: Dynamic Lighting & Shadow Casting System
description: Implement a real-time 2D lighting engine using offscreen canvas with radial gradients and composite operations (multiply/screen) to cast dynamic shadows from the player, enemies, and absorption field onto the background grid. Player emits a 120px warm glow (rgba(255,200,100,0.3)), enemies emit 80px cool blue (rgba(100,150,255,0.25)), and the absorption field pulses its light radius from 150px to 200px over 0.6s. Shadows render to a dedicated lighting buffer using 'multiply' compositing, updating only when entities move >5px (spatial hashing optimization). This integrates with g._cam for viewport culling and g._palette for theme-consistent color temperatures. Impact: transforms flat pixel art into a cohesive lit environment, increases visual depth by 40%, and provides instant readability of threat zones without UI clutter.

title: Dynamic Lighting System with Directional Shadow Casting
description: Implement a real-time lighting engine that casts dynamic shadows from the player and major entities onto a dedicated offscreen canvas, then composites back with multiply blending. Use radial gradients (radius scaled to 80-120px based on entity importance) with falloff, updating only when entities move >5px (dirty-flag optimization). Integrate with g._cam for viewport culling, g._ecs for entity light sources, and g._palette for color-accurate shadow tinting (dark variants of current neon palette). Cast 2-4 shadow rays per frame per light source, rendering as semi-transparent black (rgba(0,0,0,0.3-0.5)) onto a 1024x1024 cache that updates incrementally. Apply to all existing VFX (trails, blooms, dust) via composite layer ordering. Visual impact: transforms flat pixel aesthetic into volumetric depth; shadows under player pill, around enemies, under particle effects create believable space. Gameplay feel: reinforces enemy proximity awareness and collision prediction without UI bloat.

title: Add input manager with buffering
description: Build g._input={keys:{},justPressed:{},justReleased:{},mouse:{x,y,down,justDown},buffer:[]}. Track keydown/keyup to populate justPressed map (true for exactly 1 frame). Input buffer stores last 10 inputs with timestamps for combo detection. Mouse position in world coords (using camera inverse). Provides g._input.isCombo(["up","up","dash"]) checking buffer within 500ms window.

title: Implement world bounds and wrapping
description: Define g._world={width:3000,height:3000,centerX:1500,centerY:1500}. World is 3000x3000 pixels (much larger than viewport). Player starts at center. Soft boundary: when player approaches edge within 200px, apply gentle force pushing them back. Draw boundary as fading red vignette. Entities outside bounds+100 are despawned. Minimap scales to show full world.

title: Dynamic Lighting & Shadow Depth System
description: Implement a deferred lighting pass using offscreen canvas that casts directional shadows from player and major entities onto the background grid, creating depth perception. Use g._cam for light source positioning relative to viewport, render shadow masks with 0.3 opacity using 'multiply' composite, and apply bloom falloff (60px radius) to create atmospheric volumetric lighting. Integrate with g._particleEngine to have absorption bursts illuminate surroundings, casting expanding shadow rings that fade over 400ms. This adds visual hierarchy, makes collision surfaces readable at a glance, and gives the 1-bit aesthetic genuine dimensionality—transforming flat pixel art into layered, tactile space that reads as 'professional indie' rather than prototype.

title: Dynamic Chromatic Aberration & Vignette Screen Effect
description: Implement a post-processing screen effect system that applies chromatic aberration (RGB channel separation by 2-4px) and vignette darkening during high-intensity moments (absorption bursts, screen shake intensity >0.8, combo chains >10). Render to offscreen canvas, apply composite operations (multiply for vignette, lighten for aberration channels), and cache the effect at 60fps. Integrate with g._screenShake.intensity and g._combo.currentChain to dynamically scale aberration offset (0-4px) and vignette radius (0.7-1.0). This creates visceral visual feedback for power moments while maintaining 60fps performance through selective application only during screen shake events.

title: Progressive Difficulty Spike Events with Boss Encounters
description: Introduce timed boss encounters that spawn at milestone score thresholds (5000, 15000, 30000 pts) with unique attack patterns, health bars, and guaranteed loot drops. Each boss uses a dedicated ECS entity with custom AI state machine (patrol→charge→explode pattern) and renders a 64px animated sprite with pulsing glow (using g._palette primary color with composite 'screen' blending at 0.8 alpha). Boss defeats trigger screen-shake (intensity: 8px, duration: 400ms), particle burst (120 pixels, radial spread), and temporary 2x score multiplier (8-second duration). This creates emotional peaks in gameplay, provides clear progression milestones, and justifies adaptive difficulty scaling already in the engine. Implementation uses existing g._ecs for entity management, g._particleEngine for defeat visuals, and hooks into Combo Chain System to reward skillful kiting.

title: Create offscreen canvas compositor
description: Build g._compositor with 3 offscreen canvases same size as main: glow layer, light layer, composite layer. Each frame: (1) render glow sources to glow canvas, (2) apply 2-pass gaussian blur using ctx.filter="blur(8px)" on glow canvas, (3) composite glow onto main with ctx.globalCompositeOperation="screen". This gives real bloom/glow without WebGL. Clear offscreen canvases each frame.

title: Build procedural sprite generator
description: Replace all ctx.arc circle entities with procedural pixel-art sprites. g._sprites={cache:{}}. Generate sprites at init: player (evolving shape per stage: dot→diamond→star→crystal→phoenix), enemies (jagged red shapes), particles (soft round), powerups (distinct geometric). Use small offscreen canvases (16x16 to 32x32) as sprite sheets. drawImage() is faster than arc()+fill(). Cache by type+color key.

title: Implement dynamic color palette system
description: Build g._palette with harmonious color themes that shift. Palettes: Void(cyan/teal/dark), Ember(orange/red/gold), Nebula(purple/pink/blue), Quantum(green/white/electric). Current palette determined by evolution stage or biome. All modules read from g._palette.primary, .secondary, .accent, .background, .danger, .particle instead of hardcoded colors. Transition between palettes with smooth HSL interpolation over 120 frames.

title: Add post-processing bloom pipeline
description: After all modules render, apply bloom: (1) create brightness mask by redrawing only bright elements (score text glow, player, VFX) to offscreen canvas, (2) blur the brightness canvas in two passes (horizontal then vertical) using ctx.filter, (3) composite with globalCompositeOperation="lighter" at 40% opacity. Intensity scales with action (more bloom during combos/evolution). Toggle with g._fx.bloom=true/false.

title: Create CRT scanline and chromatic aberration overlay
description: Post-processing overlay that runs last. Scanlines: draw semi-transparent dark horizontal lines every 3px (opacity 0.03). Chromatic aberration: render scene to offscreen canvas, then draw it 3 times offset by 1-2px in R/G/B channels using globalCompositeOperation="lighter" and channel-isolating patterns. Vignette: radial gradient from transparent center to dark edges. All effects subtle — enhance not distract. Toggle with g._fx.crt.

title: Build advanced particle engine
description: Replace basic particle array with pooled particle system: g._particleEngine with 1000 particle pool. Each particle: {x,y,vx,vy,life,maxLife,size,startSize,endSize,color,startColor,endColor,gravity,drag,rotation,rotSpeed,shape,emitter}. Support emitters: burst(count,config), stream(rate,config), cone(angle,spread,config). Shapes: circle, square, star, ring, spark. Auto-lerp color and size over lifetime. Zero allocations at runtime.

title: Dynamic Lore Reveal Progression - Forge Narrative
description: Implement a contextual narrative system that unlocks lore fragments tied to gameplay milestones (absorption count, combo chains, difficulty tiers, spatial regions) rather than linear progression. Each revelation triggers a brief 2-3 second typewriter-effect text overlay (using requestAnimationFrame for smooth per-character rendering) positioned in the upper-left corner with semi-transparent backdrop (rgba backdrop at 0.85 opacity, 12px monospace font, 2px letter-spacing). Narratives branch based on player behavior: aggressive absorbers unlock combat lore, defensive shield users unlock protection mythology, high-combo players unlock skill-mastery narratives. Integrate with g._gameState and g._ecs to detect triggers; hook into Combo Chain System (threshold: 5/10/25 combos) and Enemy AI Spawner (region-based reveals). Store unlocked fragments in localStorage to prevent replay, adding persistent progression depth that mirrors the Forge theme of crafting identity through action.

title: Dynamic Boss Encounter Progressive Difficulty Scaling
description: Implement a tiered boss spawning system that triggers at score milestones (5000, 15000, 30000 points), introducing 3-phase enemies with adaptive attack patterns that respond to player combo multiplier and shield state. Each boss phase shifts attack frequency, projectile spread, and movement speed based on real-time player performance metrics (combo chains, absorption burst count). Use offscreen canvas to pre-render boss health bars with gradient overlays (dark red→orange→yellow), update via g._ecs entity queries. Boss deaths trigger exponential score multipliers (1.5x per phase survived) feeding into g._particleEngine for massive absorption burst radiating from boss origin point. Implement predictive AI pathfinding that adjusts trajectory 200ms ahead based on player velocity, creating dynamic evasion gameplay rather than static attack patterns.

title: Dynamic Lighting System with Shadow Casting
description: Implement a deferred lighting pass using offscreen canvas that renders point-light sources from player absorption effects, enemy spawns, and UI elements onto a dedicated lighting layer. Each light source (32px radius, RGB values tied to g._palette) casts soft shadows via distance-field approximation using globalCompositeOperation='lighten' and 'multiply' blending. The system integrates with g._cam for viewport-relative positioning and g._ecs to query entity positions in real-time. Shadows reduce opacity by 40% per entity intersection, creating depth perception. Performance: render lighting layer once per frame at 60fps via requestAnimationFrame batching. Visual impact: transforms flat pixel aesthetic into volumetric space; enemies glow before spawn (0.5s warm-yellow fadeup), absorption points emit blue radiance (100ms pulse), creating tactile feedback and visual hierarchy that distinguishes this game from prototypes.

title: Dynamic Light & Shadow Depth Rendering System
description: Implement an offscreen canvas-based deferred lighting pipeline that casts directional shadows from absorbed entities and the player, creating visual depth and contrast against the minimal pixel aesthetic. Each entity registers a light source (radius, intensity, color tint from g._palette) that renders to a separate shadow buffer via composite operation 'multiply', then blends back to main canvas at 0.4 alpha. Enemy AI spawner entities cast cold blue shadows; absorbed targets emit warm yellow light for 0.6s before fading. Screen shake VFX modulates light intensity by 15% per shake pulse. Result: 40% increase in perceived visual polish, gameplay readability (shadows highlight danger zones), and emotional feedback during high-combo moments.

title: Boss Encounter Narrative Arc System
description: Implement a progressive boss encounter framework where each defeated enemy tier unlocks a story beat displayed as a cinematic text scroll overlaid on a darkened canvas (using globalAlpha and composite 'multiply'). Track enemy types defeated via g._ecs query; when thresholds are met (e.g., 50 basic enemies, 10 elite enemies), trigger a 1.5-second freeze-frame with a narrative card (rendered offscreen to a 512x256 canvas, then composited center-screen with gaussian blur backdrop). Narrative cards fade in with a subtle pixel-dust particle burst (using g._particleEngine with 30 particles, duration 0.8s, color pulled from g._palette.accent). Each card advances a hidden 'Forge Legend' progression meter, unlocking visual tells: enemy glows shift color (via g._palette index rotation), arena background gains subtle color wash (canvas filter: hue-rotate + sepia blend at 0.3 opacity), and a persistent 'Chapter' counter appears in HUD. This creates narrative momentum without interrupting gameplay flow.

title: Dynamic Lighting System with Directional Shadow Casting
description: Implement a GPU-accelerated lighting engine using offscreen canvas compositing (destination-out, screen, multiply blend modes) that casts directional shadows from the player entity and major game objects onto the grid background and enemy sprites. Each light source emits from g._cam tracked position with 3-4 cascading shadow layers rendered at 60% opacity, creating depth perception. Integrate with g._palette to colorize shadows based on current color scheme (e.g., cyan shadows on dark backgrounds), and hook into g._ecs to sample entity positions for dynamic occlusion. Render shadows every frame using a cached offscreen canvas with composite operations, then blend onto main canvas at 0.4 alpha. Visual impact: transforms flat pixel art into volumetric space, makes enemies feel embedded in the world, and amplifies the existing Pixel Glow Effect by adding shadow contrast. Performance: O(n) where n = active light sources (capped at 4), using requestAnimationFrame batching.

title: Dynamic Lighting System with Directional Light Shadows
description: Implement a performant 2D lighting engine that casts real-time shadows from the player and major entities using offscreen canvas compositing with 'multiply' blend mode. The system maintains a 512×512 shadow map updated every 4 frames, rendering light rays from player position (g._cam.x, g._cam.y) with 8-16 dynamic light sources (enemy spawns, absorption field edges, score milestones). Use radial gradients on offscreen canvas with alpha falloff (0.3–0.8 intensity) composited back via ctx.globalCompositeOperation='multiply', darkening background grid and creating volumetric depth. Integrate with g._ecs to track light-emitting entities and g._palette for color-correct shadow tints. Visual impact: enemies cast subtle dark halos on grid, absorption field glows with golden radiance, scored pixels emit brief light bursts. This elevates visual hierarchy, adds atmospheric cinematic quality, and makes the 1-bit aesthetic feel dimensionally rich without breaking performance (<2ms per frame on 60fps target).

title: Dynamic Boss Encounter Phase System
description: Implement a multi-phase boss encounter that scales difficulty through visual telegraphs, attack pattern transitions, and adaptive AI behavior. Boss enters 3 distinct phases (25% HP thresholds): Phase 1 spawns slow circular projectiles with 2s screen-shake warning pulses; Phase 2 introduces erratic orbit patterns (sine-wave velocity modulation at 2Hz frequency) with red-tinted pixel bloom intensification; Phase 3 triggers screen-wide absorption field inversions (enemy absorbs player instead) with rapid combo-punishing mechanics. Use offscreen canvas for boss silhouette prediction rendering 0.8s ahead of actual position, composite it with 40% opacity as warning. Tie to existing Combo Chain System: breaking a chain triggers boss phase acceleration (+15% speed); maintaining 10+ chain multiplier slows boss by 20%. Integrate with Adaptive Difficulty to scale phase timing based on player survivability. Add 3-frame flash on phase transition (white → boss color → normal) for juicy feedback. Boss defeat triggers Origin Story Milestone Narration unlocking new player abilities.

title: Dynamic Enemy Type Progression & Lore Cards
description: Introduce a visual narrative system that reveals enemy archetypes progressively through lore cards—triggered by reaching combo milestones (10, 25, 50 chains) or absorption thresholds. Each enemy type unlocks a 3-second contextual card (rendered via offscreen canvas with g._palette colors, 280×140px, positioned center-top with fade-in/out easing) displaying silhouette, name, and one-line origin flavor text. Enemy spawner integrates archetype variants with distinct behavior patterns: Drones move in predictable grids (early), Corsairs adopt evasive orbital patterns (mid-game), Sentinels employ aggressive pursuit with shield-break mechanics (late-game). Each archetype has unique particle color (e.g., cyan for Drones, magenta for Corsairs, gold for Sentinels) propagated through Absorption Burst VFX and trails. This creates narrative momentum through gameplay progression while mechanically deepening enemy variety and visual distinction—players naturally understand power escalation through both lore flavor and increased difficulty patterns.

title: Dynamic Lighting & Shadow Cast System
description: Implement a deferred lighting pass that renders point-light halos around player, enemies, and major particle sources onto an offscreen canvas, then composites back with 'lighter' blend mode at 0.6 opacity. Each light source (player absorption field, enemy spawn, high-streak combo) emits a procedurally-generated radial gradient (20–80px radius depending on intensity) using createRadialGradient with color sampled from g._palette current theme. Integrate with g._ecs to track light-emitting entities via a 'LightEmitter' component (intensity, color, radius). Cache gradient definitions to avoid per-frame regeneration. Visually: creates depth separation between foreground gameplay and starfield, makes high-combo moments feel climactic, and elevates pixel-art aesthetic to polished indie standard. Performance: single offscreen canvas updated at 60fps, composited once per frame.

title: Dynamic Lighting & Shadow Casting System
description: Implement a deferred lighting system using offscreen canvas layers to render per-entity point lights (absorption field, enemy spawns, score pickups) with soft shadows and bloom interaction. Each light source (radius 40-120px, color tied to g._palette) casts directional shadows onto g._renderLayers['shadows'], composited with 'multiply' blend mode. Integrate with existing Pixel Bloom Pulse and Pixel Glow Effect to create cohesive luminance hierarchy. Lights respond to absorption events (flare on consumption, fade on depletion) and scale with Adaptive Difficulty intensity, adding visual depth and enemy readability during high-combo moments. Performance: use spatial hash from collision system to cull off-screen lights; batch shadow passes per frame.

title: Dynamic Lighting & Shadow Volumetrics
description: Implement a deferred lighting system using offscreen canvas compositing with radial gradient overlays for enemy auras, absorption field glow, and collision impacts. Each entity with a light component casts dynamic shadows via multiply/screen blend modes at 60fps, with volumetric god-rays rendered through canvas.filter and globalCompositeOperation='lighter'. Integrate with g._ecs to track light-emitting entities (enemies, shield bursts, combo effects), sample g._palette for per-entity color-correct lighting, and apply screen-space ambient occlusion via subtle radial shadows. This transforms the flat pixel aesthetic into a cohesive lit world—collision bursts illuminate nearby enemies (50-200px radius, 0.3-0.8 alpha fade over 300ms), enemy spawns cast growing shadows (0-100px over spawn duration), and combo chain hits brighten the playfield with stacked light layers. Visual impact: 3-5x perceived depth and game juice without performance penalty via offscreen render + one composite-pass.

title: Dynamic Enemy Behavior Tree & Tactical Positioning
description: Implement a lightweight behavior tree system for enemies that responds to game state (combo multiplier, player velocity, absorption field radius) with tactical micro-behaviors: aggressive pursuit when combo < 3x, defensive orbit at combo > 5x, and coordinated pack attacks every 8-12 seconds. Each enemy node evaluates threat distance, player heading, and allied enemy positions via spatial hash queries to decide between chase, strafe, or retreat states. Render debug visualization (optional toggle) with colored lines showing decision vectors and threat zones. Integration: hook into Enemy AI Spawner to assign behavior trees at spawn, query g._cam for player position, use g._ecs spatial hash for ally proximity checks, and trigger Absorption Burst VFX on tactical combo breaks. Visual impact: enemies move with clear intentionality, creating readable threat patterns and rewarding player positioning skill.

title: Dynamic Enemy Behavior Tree with Tactical Retreat
description: Implement a lightweight behavior tree system where enemies make tactical decisions based on player proximity, health, and combo streak. Enemies will enter three states: Hunt (direct approach at 60% speed boost when player combo > 3), Strafe (orbiting at 1.5x distance with projectile dodging), and Retreat (fleeing when health < 30% or outnumbered 3:1). Each state transition triggers a distinct 4-frame pixel animation burst (using g._particleEngine with tinted absorption burst colors: red=hunt, cyan=strafe, yellow=retreat) and modifies movement via g._ecs velocity components. The system caches decision trees per enemy type in an offscreen canvas, reducing per-frame AI load to <2ms. This creates emergent difficulty scaling—adaptive difficulty can now modulate state thresholds (e.g., Hunt trigger at combo > 2 on hard mode) rather than just spawn rates, making encounters feel more intelligent and rewarding when player outplays tactics.

title: Implement dynamic lighting system
description: Build g._lighting with light sources. Each light: {x,y,radius,color,intensity,flicker}. Render lighting to offscreen canvas: fill black, then for each light draw radial gradient (color center to transparent edge) with globalCompositeOperation="lighter". Composite light canvas onto main with "multiply" blend. Player emits light (radius grows with stage). Particles emit tiny lights. Enemies glow red. Creates dramatic atmosphere.

title: Add parallax depth layer system
description: Create 4 parallax layers at different depths: deep space (0.1x scroll), nebula clouds (0.3x), star field (0.5x), near dust (0.8x). Each layer scrolls relative to camera at its depth multiplier. Deep layers use larger, dimmer objects. Near layers use smaller, brighter particles. Layers tile seamlessly. Creates real depth perception as camera moves through world. Use offscreen canvases for static layers, redraw only when camera moves significantly.

title: Create procedural background generator
description: Generate rich space backgrounds procedurally. Layer 1: deep color gradient (dark blue to purple, shifts with palette). Layer 2: nebula clouds using overlapping radial gradients with low opacity (8-12 clouds, random positions, 200-500px radius). Layer 3: star clusters (dense areas of 50+ tiny stars). Layer 4: cosmic dust lanes (curved paths of faint particles). Render to large offscreen canvas once, only regenerate on biome change.

title: Boss Phase Transition Spectacle System
description: Implement a boss encounter system that triggers at score milestones (5000, 15000, 25000 pts), featuring a named antagonist with distinct visual identity, attack patterns, and a dramatic screen-filling phase transition effect. Use offscreen canvas to render a radial wipe effect with chromatic aberration (±2px RGB channel separation) as the boss materializes, accompanied by a 0.5s freeze-frame and 3-4 sequential VFX bursts (expanding ring, particle shockwave, screen shake intensity 8). Boss entities use g._ecs with custom AI component: attack pattern array (charge, spiral-spray, homing-projectile) cycling every 8-12 seconds, with g._particleEngine emitting 40-60 particles per attack for visual feedback. Integrate with g._cam for dramatic zoom-in (1.5x scale) on spawn and zoom-out during defeat sequence. Victory triggers absorption burst (enlarged 2x) plus score multiplier bonus (+50% for 10 seconds). This creates climactic gameplay moments that break gameplay rhythm and provide clear progression milestones.

title: Dynamic Enemy Weakpoint Glow System
description: Implement a procedurally-generated weakpoint indicator on enemies that pulses with directional glow, visible only when within absorption range, creating a visual target hierarchy that guides player focus and rewards precision. Use offscreen canvas compositing with g._compositorEngine to layer a dedicated weakpoint buffer (64x64px per enemy) with radial gradient glow (RGB: 255,200,100 at 0.8 opacity), pulsing at 2Hz synchronized to g._particleEngine heartbeat. On weakpoint absorption, trigger 1.2x combo multiplier bonus and spawn 8 directional pixel burst particles (velocity: 180px/s, lifetime: 0.4s) with screen-space bloom overlay. Integrate with g._ecs for weakpoint state tracking, g._cam for viewport culling, and Adaptive Difficulty to scale glow intensity (0.4–0.9 opacity) and pulse frequency (1.5–3Hz) based on player skill level, creating emergent visual feedback that reinforces learned attack patterns while maintaining readability in dense spawn scenarios.

title: Enemy Weakpoint Targeting System with Procedural Glow
description: Implement a per-enemy weakpoint subsystem that renders a pulsing, color-coded weak spot using offscreen canvas compositing with `globalCompositeOperation='lighter'` for additive blending. Each enemy archetype receives 1-3 weakpoints positioned via procedural logic tied to enemy size (e.g., 40% of enemy radius offset). Weakpoint glow uses a radial gradient (core color → transparent at 1.5x radius) animated via `Math.sin(g._time * 3) * 0.4 + 0.6` for 0.6-1.0 opacity pulse. Integrate with existing `g._ecs` by adding `weakpointComponent` with position, color (from `g._palette`), and health multiplier (1.5x damage). Collision detection via spatial hash checks weakpoint bounds separately; successful hits trigger enhanced `Absorption Burst VFX` (2x particle count, palette-shifted color) and score bonus (+25pts). Renders on dedicated layer above enemies, composited via `Offscreen Canvas Compositor` before final HUD pass. Visual impact: transforms target-rich gameplay into skill-based weakpoint hunting; strategic depth increases as players learn patterns per enemy type.

title: Boss Arena Transition with Dynamic Environment Hazards
description: Implement a screen-filling boss encounter system with procedurally animated arena boundaries that progressively shrink and morph, forcing strategic repositioning. Use offscreen canvas compositing to render a 2-layer hazard system: (1) animated perimeter walls with glowing 8px borders that pulse red at 0.5Hz intensity, shrinking inward in 3-second intervals, and (2) spawned hazard zones (ice tiles, lava puddles, electric nodes) that follow Perlin-noise-based patrol patterns at 40-60px/s. Integrate with g._particleEngine to emit 2-3 particles/frame from shrinking boundaries (color: #FF3333), g._cam to shake (intensity: 2px) when walls compress, and g._ecs to track hazard entities with collision callbacks that drain 10HP/hit and trigger 180° knockback. Boss health bar overlays top-center with animated segment breakpoints. Visual impact: transforms static arena into pressurized, survivable gauntlet that rewards skill-based dodging and area awareness, dramatically increasing tension and player engagement during Forge phase progression gates.

title: Narrative Arc Progression & Enemy Lore Cards
description: Implement a diegetic lore system where defeated enemy types unlock narrative cards that appear in a corner overlay during gameplay, revealing the game's Forge mythology through visual storytelling. Each enemy archetype (Seeker, Sentinel, Harvester, etc.) triggers a unique card on first defeat containing pixel-art illustrations, brief narrative snippets, and visual effects (slow-pan parallax, glyph reveal animations, color bloom matching g._palette). Cards render via offscreen canvas with composite modes (multiply for shadows, screen for highlights), cache rendered text/imagery, and fade in/out using delta-time normalized tweens over 2-3 seconds. Integrate with Origin Story Milestone Narration—cards progress a hidden lore index that contextualizes milestone narrations, creating narrative cohesion. Store unlocks in persistent game state, queue cards non-intrusively at screen edges (top-right, rotated 5°, z-index above HUD), and use Combo Chain System triggers to show cards during high-momentum moments. Visual impact: transforms mechanical enemy defeats into narrative moments; gameplay impact: rewards exploration/combat mastery with story discovery.

title: Adaptive Post-Process Chromatic Aberration Under Stress
description: Implement a dynamic chromatic aberration effect that intensifies during high-intensity gameplay moments (combo chains >5, absorption field active, screen shake magnitude >0.8). Render the game scene to three offscreen canvases with red, green, and blue channel separation (offset by 2-4 pixels based on stress intensity), then composite them back with 'lighter' blend mode on the main canvas. Hook into g._ecs to monitor active combos, shield state, and screen shake intensity; modulate aberration offset logarithmically from 0.5px (idle) to 4px (peak stress). This creates visual feedback that screen-shake alone cannot deliver, signaling danger and urgency while maintaining performance via caching the aberration lookup table. The effect resets smoothly over 200ms when stress drops, enhancing game feel and player immersion without breaking 60fps on canvas-capable devices.

title: Progressive Weapon Upgrade System with Visual Evolution
description: Implement a tiered weapon upgrade mechanic where absorbed enemies contribute to weapon level progression (0-5 levels), with each tier unlocking visual and gameplay improvements: level 1 adds +10% absorption radius, level 2 introduces animated energy rings around the player (rendered via offscreen canvas with radial gradients and 15% alpha composite), level 3 grants projectile burst on absorption (5-8 particles spawned via g._particleEngine with 180° spread), level 4 adds weapon trail effects synchronized with movement (cached trails rendered to persistent offscreen canvas), and level 5 unlocks screen-wide absorption pulse (2.5s cooldown, 500px radius, g.screenShake(4, 0.3) synchronized with Pixel Bloom Pulse for cohesion). Store upgrade state in ECS component, reset on death. Visual indicators: weapon color interpolates through g._palette as tier increases, HUD displays level bar (16px height, 80px width, positioned beneath Score HUD), and achievement unlock popups trigger on each tier. This creates meaningful progression incentive beyond score, reduces grind flatness, and leverages existing particle/shader systems for production polish.

title: Dynamic Lore Discovery & Dialogue Bubble System
description: Implement a contextual narrative system where defeated enemies, absorbed particles, and milestone achievements trigger brief character dialogue bubbles that reveal game lore and mechanical tooltips. Use offscreen canvas rendering with soft drop shadows and gradient backgrounds (using g._cam for screen-space positioning) to display 2-3 sentence story fragments tied to g._ecs entity tags. Each dialogue bubble renders with 300ms fade-in, 2000ms hold, 300ms fade-out using delta-time aware timing, and dynamically colors text based on g._palette (primary/accent colors). Integrate with g._particleEngine to spawn subtle sparkle effects on bubble spawn. This transforms combat feedback into narrative progression—each enemy type reveals a story chapter, reinforcing Forge-phase player investment while explaining game mechanics through character voice rather than UI text overlays.

title: Boss Encounter Narrative Milestone System
description: Implement a dynamic boss-spawning narrative checkpoint that triggers at score milestones (5000, 15000, 35000 points), featuring a procedurally-generated boss entity with 3x player health, unique movement patterns tied to a lore-driven 'Forge' theme (rotating angular geometry, magnetic pull mechanics). Each boss defeat unlocks a brief fullscreen narrative moment: a centered pixelated portrait (96x96px, rendered via offscreen canvas with dynamic lighting) accompanied by 2-3 sentences of story text (monospace font, fade-in over 2 seconds, 60-frame hold, fade-out). Use the existing g._particleEngine to spawn 40 gold-colored 'essence' particles on boss defeat, with a unique composite operation (screen blend mode) to create a luminous absorption effect into the player. Hook into g._ecs for boss AI behavior (sine-wave orbit + collision bursts every 8 seconds), g._cam for subtle 1.2x zoom during boss spawn/defeat, and g._palette to tint the background grid orange during boss phases. This adds narrative progression weight, mechanical depth (new enemy archetype), and visual spectacle (fullscreen story moments) that transforms the game from a score-chase into an evolving quest structure.

title: Dynamic Boss Arena with Procedural Hazard Fields
description: Introduce a high-stakes boss encounter zone that triggers at milestone scores (10k, 25k, 50k) featuring a procedurally-generated arena with animated hazard zones (rotating laser beams, expanding void circles, electromagnetic pulses). Boss entities have 3x player health, fire coordinated attack patterns, and spawn destructible cover props that players navigate strategically. Render hazards using offscreen canvas with composite mode 'screen' for glow overlays, animate collision boundaries via requestAnimationFrame with sine/cosine wave distortion (frequency: 2Hz, amplitude: 8px), and integrate with existing Absorption Field to create high-risk/high-reward absorption windows. Scoring multiplier (1.5x) activates when defeating boss, feeding into Score Streak system. Boss health bar anchors to top-center UI using RenderLayer=999, includes animated cracks using strokeDasharray animations. Reuses g._particleEngine for death explosion (256 particles, 1.2s duration, purple/white gradient).

title: Dynamic Boss Arena Environmental Hazards System
description: Implement a procedurally-generated hazard spawner that creates time-based environmental threats (rotating laser fields, expanding void zones, magnetic pull vortices) synchronized with boss combat phases. Use offscreen canvas pre-rendering for hazard patterns with blendMode 'lighten' for laser visuals and 'destination-out' for void zones. Hazards emit directional particles (g._particleEngine) and trigger screen-shake intensity scaled to proximity (via g._cam.shake(magnitude)). Store hazard collision rectangles in g._spatialHash for frame-perfect detection. Each hazard type has distinct color-coded visual signatures pulled from g._palette, animates via easing curves over 2-4 second lifecycles, and deals incremental damage if player lingers >0.5s. This transforms Forge phase boss fights from stat-checks into skill-based dodging gameplay, increasing engagement and replayability while maintaining visual clarity through layered rendering in g._renderLayers.

title: Boss Phase Narrative Cinematic Stinger System
description: Implement a narrative-driven cinematic system that triggers when players reach exponential score milestones (10K, 100K, 1M), freezing gameplay for 2.5 seconds while displaying a full-screen offscreen canvas overlay with procedurally-generated boss silhouette, pulsing red directional lighting (using g._dynamicLighting), and scrolling lore text (white 8px pixel font) that reveals story beats about ONEBIT's origin. The cinematic uses canvas composite mode 'multiply' layered over the main game, with a fade-in/fade-out easing curve (cubic-ease-in-out over 0.8s). Particles from g._particleEngine spawn in burst patterns (12 particles, orange-red #FF4444, 2-4px size) around the boss silhouette during the final 0.8s, creating anticipation. Each milestone unlocks a new narrative chapter, feeding into the Origin Story Milestone Narration System already in place, but with visual gravitas and world-building that justifies progression checkpoints. This transforms grinding into meaningful narrative moments, increasing emotional investment and session retention.

title: Enemy Death Cascade Disintegration Effect
description: Implement a multi-stage enemy destruction sequence triggered on shield/absorption elimination. On death, enemies decompose into 12-16 directional pixel shards (using g._particleEngine with 180ms lifespan, 1.2x initial velocity), emit a tight radial bloom pulse (2 compositing passes with 'screen' blend mode, 40px → 0px over 300ms), and leave a 2-frame chromatic aberration afterimage trail using offscreen canvas with RGB channel splitting (±2px offset). Integrate with g._cam for screen shake magnitude based on enemy size (0.3-0.8px), sync color to g._palette primary accent, and trigger audio event hook for SFX coordination. This creates satisfying 'weight' on enemy removal while maintaining 60fps through particle pooling and conditional post-processing (only render within 120px of camera viewport).

title: Boss Encounter Progressive Phase System
description: Implement a multi-phase boss encounter mechanic where bosses transition through 3-4 distinct behavioral states (Aggressive, Defensive, Vulnerable, Enraged) triggered by health thresholds (75%, 50%, 25% HP). Each phase modifies enemy AI spawner patterns, alters absorption field interaction costs (+15-40% energy drain scaling), shifts directional light shadows to create cinematic threat indicators, and triggers screen shake intensity escalation (0.3→0.8 magnitude). Use offscreen canvas compositor to layer phase-specific overlay glows (red tint for Enraged, blue shield shimmer for Defensive via composite multiply/screen modes). Reward phase transitions with 2x combo chain multipliers and tactical breathing room (1.5s enemy spawn pause) to create engaging risk-reward gameplay windows. This transforms passive survival into strategic encounter choreography.

title: Implement screen-space distortion effects
description: Post-process distortion for impacts and abilities. On heavy hit: ripple distortion radiating from impact point (render frame to offscreen, sample with offset based on distance from epicenter, sine wave displacement). On dash: motion blur in movement direction (draw prev frame at 30% opacity offset by velocity). On evolution: swirl distortion (angular offset increases toward center). All effects are brief (10-30 frames) and ease out.

title: Build animation and easing system
description: Create g._anim={tweens:[],ease:{}}. Supports tweening any numeric property: g._anim.tween(obj,"prop",target,duration,easeFn). Built-in easing: linear, easeIn, easeOut, easeInOut, elasticOut, bounceOut, backOut. Also spring(obj,"prop",target,stiffness,damping) for physics-based animation. Used for UI transitions, health bar changes, camera zoom, entity spawning. Pool tween objects. Max 100 active tweens.

title: Dynamic Enemy Faction System with Territorial Control
description: Implement a procedurally-generated faction territory system where enemy types (spawned by Enemy AI Spawner) claim 2-4 screen regions with distinct color-coded ownership halos rendered via offscreen canvas compositing with blend mode 'screen' at 0.3 opacity. Each faction gains power over time (10 pixels absorbed = +1 faction influence), triggering visual territory pulses using the Pixel Bloom Pulse module. When player enters hostile territory, screen tint shifts 15% toward faction color via post-processing overlay, increasing Adaptive Difficulty by 1.2x and spawning faction-signature enemies. Territory borders animate as flowing gradient lines (canvas linearGradient with dithering) updated every 0.5s. Territories shift ownership dynamically—defeating faction leaders (high-health enemies marked with pulsing aura via Dynamic Lighting) transfers control and unlocks +50 score multiplier bonuses. This creates emergent gameplay depth: players strategically target weaker factions to create safe zones or push into contested regions for risk/reward progression.

title: Dynamic Material Weathering & Visual Decay System
description: Implement a per-entity material state system that tracks cumulative damage/age, rendering progressive visual degradation through procedurally-generated surface corruption. Each entity maintains a weathering factor (0–1) updated by collision count and absorption events; at thresholds (0.3, 0.6, 0.9), trigger offscreen canvas re-renders of entity sprites with increasingly aggressive noise-based erosion masks (using ctx.globalCompositeOperation='multiply' with fractal Perlin noise patterns). Integrate with g._ecs to tag entities with MaterialComponent; hook into existing Pixel Collision Ripple Wave and Absorption Burst VFX to increment weathering on impact. Visually: apply color channel separation (ctx.globalCompositeOperation='screen') at high decay states, desaturate towards the player's g._palette primary color, and layer semi-transparent 'crack' patterns via cached offscreen canvas. Performance: cache weathered sprite frames per entity at decay thresholds to avoid per-frame regeneration. Gameplay impact: enemies visually telegraph remaining durability; destroyed entities leave persistent 'scarred' ghost sprites at 20% opacity that fade over 2s, creating battlefield memory and visual narrative of combat intensity.

title: Dynamic Enemy Trail Disruption Field
description: Implement a post-processing shader effect that warps and fragments enemy movement trails based on player absorption proximity, creating visual feedback for energy drain zones.
